How does the ping command work by default?


 
A. Is sends an ICMP Echo Request to a remote host and waits to receive an ICMP Echo Response in return.

 
B. It sends an ARP request to a remote host and waits to receive an ARP response in return.

 
C. It sends a TCP SYN packet to a remote host and waits to receive an TCP ACK response in return.

 
D. Is sends a broadcast packet to all hosts on the net and waits to receive, among others, a response from the target system.

 
E. It sends a UDP packet to port 0 of the remote host and waits to receive a UDP error response in return.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: A
Comment

A. 203.0.113.64

 
B. 203.0.113.78

 
C. 203.0.113.65

 
D. 203.0.113.80

 
E. 203.0.113.81

the correct answer is B and C./28 = 16 bits for the hosts= 16 adresses -2 (network + broadcast)= 14 available hosts.hostMin = 1hostMax = 142

I meant: /28 = 4 bits remaining for the hosts that equals 16 adresses.1

/28 = 255.255.255.240 = 7 ip´sonly C6

ops = 15 ip´s validos, B e C mesmo52






Which mechanism does ssh use to interact with the SSH agent?


 
A. Connecting to port 2222 which is used by the system-wide SSH agent.

 
B. Using the fixed socket .ssh-agent/ipc.

 
C. Creating an alias replacing ssh with calls to ssh-agent.

 
D. Starting ssh-agent as a child process for each ssh invocation.

 
E. Evaluating environment variables such as SSH_AUTH_SOCK.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: E
Comment

Why the h3ll is this important for a Linux administrator. It’s just like asking someone at their drivers test how an internal combustion engine works.51

Because you’ll be the guy who troubleshoots this, if it is not working 😉






Which of the following commands finds all files owned by root that have the SetUID bit set?


 
A. find / -user root -perm -4000

 
B. find / -user 0 -mode +s

 
C. find / -owner root -setuid

 
D. find / -owner 0 -permbits 0x100000000

 
E. find / –filter uid=1 –filter pers=u+s

What is true regarding public and private SSH keys? (Choose two.)


 
A. For each user account, there is exactly one key pair that can be used to log into that account.

 
B. The private key must never be revealed to anyone.

 
C. Several different public keys may be generated for the same private key.

 
D. To maintain the private key’s confidentiality, the SSH key pair must be created by its owner.

 
E. To allow remote logins, the user’s private key must be copied to the remote server.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: BD
Comment




How do shadow passwords improve the password security in comparison to standard no-shadow password?


 
A. Regular users do not have access to the password hashes of shadow passwords.

 
B. Every shadow password is valid for 45 days and must be changed afterwards.

 
C. The system’s host key is used to encrypt all shadow passwords.

 
D. Shadow passwords are always combined with a public key that has to match the user’s private key.

 
E. Shadow passwords are stored in plain text and can be checked for weak passwords.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: A
Comment

What is true regarding the statement beginning with #! that is found in the first line of script? (Choose two.)


 
A. It prevents the scripts from being executed until the ! is removed.

 
B. it triggers the installation of the script’s interpreter.

 
C. It specifies the path and the arguments of the interpreter used to run the script.

 
D. It defines the character encoding of the script.

 
E. It is a comment that is ignored by the script interpreter.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: BC
Comment

so is it C, or both C and E????????“Many scripting languages, such as Perl, use the # character to indicate the beginning of a comment line that should be ignored by the interpreter. The shebang is a special exception to this rule.” -output from a random google search.Im leaning towards just C (and only C), why does this stupid question say to choose two.Also, the test dump that I originally got this question from, has it as B and C (which is most likely wrong).Your welcome; Im glad I can share my confusion with you.Happy studying!22

I think JOhn explains it right, get ready for “stupid” questions, they will be like this on the real test 😉1

C and E,the followed phrase is from wikipedia “Shebang (Unix)”“The shebang line is usually ignored by the interpreter, because the “#” character is a comment marker in many scripting languages; some language interpreters that do not use the hash mark to begin comments still may ignore the shebang line in recognition of its purpose.”1

only C

C and E87

title: 【LPIC-1】Exam Question LPI-102 (Exam 3)
content:
Which of the following is true about IPv6?


 
A. IPv6 no longer supports broadcast addresses.

 
B. With IPv6, the TCP port numbers of most services have changed.

 
C. IPv4 addresses can be used without any change with IPv6.

 
D. IPv6 no longer supports multicast addresses.

 
E. For IPv6, UDP and TCP have been replaced by the Rapid Transmission Protocol RTP.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: A
Comment




After editing the TCP wrapper configuration to grant specific hosts access to a service, when do these changes become effective?


 
A. The new configuration becomes effective after restarting the respective service.

 
B. The new configuration becomes effective at the next system reboot.

 
C. The new configuration becomes effective when the last established connection to the service is closed.

 
D. The new configuration becomes effective after restarting the tcpd service.

 
E. The new configuration becomes effective immediately for all new connections.





Click to show answer
Correct Answer: E
Comment

TCP-wrapped services do not cache the rules from the hosts access files, so any changes to hosts.allow or hosts.deny take effect immediately, without restarting network services.1

E is ok7

A213

C is correct.Man dig -t typeThis option indicates the resource record type to query, which can be any valid query type. If it is a resource record type supported in BIND 9, it can be given by the type mnemonic (such as NS or AAAA). The default query type is A, unless the -x option is supplied to indicate a reverse lookup. A zone transfer can be requested by specifying a type of AXFR. When an incremental zone transfer (IXFR) is required , set the type to ixfr=N. The incremental zone transfer contains all changes made to the zone since the serial number in the zone’s SOA record was N. All resource record types can be expressed as TYPEnn, where nn is the number of the type. If the resource record type is not supported in BIND 9, the result is displayed as described in RFC 3597.

title: Plan for studying for the TOEIC and achieving a score of 800 within 5 months using SMART goals
content:
SMARTS - Specific: To achieve a score of 800 on the TOEIC exam in 5 months.
M - Measurable: The goal is measurable as the score of 800 can be verified on the TOEIC score report.
A - Achievable: A score of 800 on the TOEIC is achievable with proper study and preparation.
R - Relevant: Improving your score on the TOEIC is relevant if you need the score for academic or professional purposes.
T - Time-bound: The goal is time-bound, with a deadline of 5 months.
Step by StepStep 1: Assess your current level: Take a practice TOEIC test to determine your current score and identify areas for improvement.
Step 2: Create a study schedule: Set aside dedicated time for TOEIC study each week, and stick to the schedule. Aim to study for at least 2 hours a day, 5 days a week.
Step 3: Study materials: Use a combination of study materials, including textbooks, practice tests, and online resources, to enhance your learning. Focus on areas where you need improvement, such as grammar and vocabulary.
Step 4: Practice, practice, practice: Regular practice is key to success on the TOEIC. Take as many practice tests as you can, and review your results to identify areas for improvement.
Step 5: Review and refinement: Regularly review what you have learned and refine your study strategies as needed. Seek feedback from a tutor or study group to identify areas for improvement.
Step 6: Take the TOEIC: After 5 months of dedicated study and preparation, take the TOEIC exam with confidence, knowing you have done everything possible to achieve a score of 800.

title: Tạo mục tiêu và hành động "Pass chứng chỉ LPIC-1 với OKRs"
content:
Mục đích
Thực hiện đạt chứng chỉ LPI LPIC-1
Nâng cao khả năng của mình trong lĩnh vực công nghệ thông tin
Tăng cơ hội nghề nghiệp trong tương lai

OKRsKế hoạch của tôi để đạt được chứng chỉ LPIC-1 bằng cách sử dụng OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) sẽ như sau:
Mục tiêu chung:Đạt được chứng chỉ LPIC-1 trong thời gian 12 tháng.
Các mục tiêu cụ thể:
Học các kiến thức cơ bản về Linux: Thực hiện học tập và làm bài tập thực hành về các chủ đề như quản lý hệ thống, quản lý tài nguyên, quản lý mạng, cấu hình và quản lý dịch vụ trong Linux trong thời gian 6 tháng.

Chuẩn bị cho kỳ thi LPIC-1: Thực hiện luyện tập và ôn tập các kiến thức đã học, cũng như làm các bài tập và bài thi mô phỏng trong thời gian 6 tháng.


Kết quả cụ thể:
Tỷ lệ hoàn thành tốt các chủ đề trong quá trình học: 80% trong 6 tháng.
Điểm trung bình trong các bài tập và bài thi mô phỏng: 85% trong 6 tháng.
Tỷ lệ thành công trong kỳ thi LPIC-1: 90% trong thời gian 12 tháng.

Sử dụng OKRs sẽ giúp tôi có một kế hoạch chi tiết và rõ ràng để đạt được mục tiêu của mình
Hành động
Tìm kiếm và tải tài liệu về LPI LPIC-1
Đăng ký và tham gia các khóa học trực tuyến
Tạo kế hoạch luyện tập và thực hành mỗi tuần
Đăng ký tham gia kỳ thi LPI LPIC-1
Theo dõi và đánh giá tiến độ của mình theo OKRs.

dpkgdpkg --i(--install) tên gói: gói cài đặt

title: Linux - Tổng hợp câu lệnh Linux
content:
Linux - df / du / fsck / e2fsck / tune2fs / dumpe2fs / XFS / ReiserFS
◆ Managing file systems
File system free space and inode usage aredfYou can check it with the command. In Linux, the following two areIt is stored separately. (2) is stored ininodeIt is an administrative area called . inode is the file systemIt is automatically prepared when creating files and directories and is used one by one when creating. to disk spaceEven if there is space, if a large number of small files are generated and there are insufficient inodes, new files cannot be generated.
(1) File data(2) File attributes and management information
◆ Syntax : df [ Options ] [ Device Name / Directory ]
option	explanation-i	View inode usage-h	Displays capacity in units (MB and GB)-k	Capacity displayed in KB-m	Capacity displayed in MB
　
　
To view the capacity of a directory or file, clickducommand. Directory name in the argumentIf not displayed, the current directory is displayed.
◆ Syntax : du [ Option ] [ Device Name / Directory ]
option	explanation-a	Displays not only directories, but also file capacity-c	Total capacity is also displayed-h	Convert capacity to units (MB or GB)-k	Capacity displayed in KB-m	Capacity displayed in MB-s	Show totals for only specified files and directories-S	Show totals without including capacity in subdirectories
　
【 Difference between df and du 】command	meaning	explanationdf	DiskFree	Viewing file system free spacedu	DiskUsed	View how much space files in a directory are using
◆ Checking and repairing file systems
If a file system failure causes a system failure,fsckThe command allows the file system toYou can attempt to check the integrity and repair the failure. Disk Check and Repair File Systemin the unmounted state. * If you execute it on a disk that is being written, the file system may be corrupted.
◆ Syntax: fsck [ option ] device name
option	explanation-t	Specifying the file system type (ext2, ext3, xfs, etc.)-a	Perform repairs automatically-r	Perform remediation interactively-A	Checking all filesystems listed in /etc/fstab-N	Shows what the command does without actually repairing
◆ Execution example: Check /dev/sda1 in ext3 file system　# fsck -t ext3 -a /dev/sda1
The ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems use the e2fsck command to check and repair file systems.
◆ Syntax: e2fsck [ option ] device name
option	explanation-c	Checking for bad blocks-p	Automatically repairs all bad blocks-v	Show more information-y	Automatically answer “yes” to all inquiries-n	Automatically answer “no” to all inquiries
◆ Execution example: Check /dev/sda1 in ext3 file system　# e2fsck -y /dev/sda1
◆ File system tuning
tune2fsThe command allows you to set various parameters of ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems.In addition to setting parameters, you can also convert file systems from ext2 format to ext3 format.
◆ Syntax: tune2fs [ option ] device name
option	explanation-c count	Specify the maximum number of times a file system can be mounted without checking it-i time	Specifying the File System Check Interval

j	Converting a file system from ext2 format to ext3 format
L	Setting the volume label for a file system

◆ Execution example: Converting the /dev/sda5 file system from ext2 format to ext3 format　# tune2fs -j /dev/sda5
dumpe2fsThe command displays various information about ext2, ext3, and ext4 file systems.
◆ Syntax : dumpe2fs [ option ] device name
◆ Execution example: Display the contents of the superblock of /dev/sda1　# dumpe2fs -h /dev/sda1
◆ XFS and ReiserFS File Systems
Although many Linux distributions now have ext3 or ext4 as the standard file system,XFS and ReiserFSFile system is also available. Each file system has its own commands.
File System	explanationXFS	A file system developed by SGI. Suitable for handling large files.ReiserFS	A journaling file system developed for Linux. Fast file search.
【 Main commands used in XFS file system 】command	explanationmkfs.xfs	Creating an XFS file system (command invoked with mkfs -t)xfs_info	View XFS file system informationxfs_db	Perform XFS file system debuggingxfs_check	Check XFS file systemxfsdump	XFS File System Backup
【 Main commands used in ReiserFS file system 】command	explanationmkfs.reiserfs	Creating a ReiserFS file system (mkreiserfs, same for mkfs -t reiserfs)reiserfsck	ReiserFS File System Checksdebugreiserfs	Troubleshooting ReiserFS File System Problemsreiserfstune	Adjust ReiserFS file system parameters

title: LPI 101-500
content:
Which type of file system is created by mkfs when it is executed with the block device name only and without any additional parameters?

A. XFS
B. VFAT
C. ext2
D. ext3
E. ext4








Correct Answer: C



Which umask value ensures that new directories can be read, written and listed by their owning user, read and listed by their owning group and are not accessible at all for everyone else?

A. 0750
B. 0027
C. 0036
D. 7640
E. 0029








Correct Answer:



Which of the following commands changes the number of days before the ext3 filesystem on /dev/sda1 has to run through a full filesystem check while booting?

A. tune2fs -d 200 /dev/sda1
B. tune2fs -i 200 /dev/sda1
C. tune2fs -c 200 /dev/sda1
D. tune2fs -n 200 /dev/sda1
E. tune2fs –days 200 /dev/sda1








Correct Answer: B



Which is the default percentage of reserved space for the root user on new ext4 filesystems?

A. 10%
B. 3%
C. 15%
D. 0%
E. 5%








Correct Answer: E



Which of the following is true when a file system, which is neither listed in /etc/fstab nor known to system, is mounted manually?

A.  A. systemd ignores any manual mounts which are not done using the systemctl mount command
B. The command systemctl mountsync can be used to create a mount unit based on the existing mount
C. systemd automatically generates a mount unit and monitors the mount point without changing it
D. Unless a systemd mount unit is created, systemd unmounts the file system after a short period of time
E. systemctl unmount must be used to remove the mount because system opens a file descriptor on the mount point








Correct Answer: C



Which program updates the database that is used by the locate command? (Specify ONLY the command without any path or parameters).









Correct Answer: updatedb

What does the command mount –bind do?

A. It makes the contents of one directory available in another directory
B. It mounts all available filesystems to the current directory
C. It mounts all user mountable filesystems to the user’s home directory
D. It mounts all file systems listed in /etc/fstab which have the option userbind set
E. It permanently mounts a regular file to a directory








Correct Answer: A



Consider the following output from the command ls `”i: How would a new file named c.txt be created with the same inode number as a.txt (Inode 525385)? 
 
 A. ln -h a.txt c.txt- 
 B. ln c.txt a.txt- 
 C. ln a.txt c.txt- 
 D. ln -f c.txt a.txt- 
 E. ln -i 525385 c.txt
Consider the following directory: drwxrwxr-x 2 root sales 4096 Jan 1 15:21 sales Which command ensures new files created within the directory sales are owned by the group sales? (Choose two.)
 A. chmod g+s sales- 
 B. setpol -R newgroup=sales sales- 
 C. chgrp -p sales sales- 
 D. chown --persistent *.sales sales- 
 E. chmod 2775 sales
 









Correct Answer: AE
 

In order to display all currently mounted filesystems, which of the following commands could be used? (Choose two.)
A. cat /proc/self/mounts- 
B. free- 
C. lsmounts- 
D. mount- 
E. cat /proc/filesystems










Correct Answer: AD

Which command displays the current disk space usage for all mounted file systems? (Specify ONLY the command without any path or parameters.)









Correct Answer: du

Which chown command changes the ownership to dave and the group to staff on a file named data.txt?
A. chown dave/staff data.txt- 
B. chown -u dave -g staff data.txt- 
C. chown --user dave --group staff data.txt- 
D. chown dave+staff data.txt- 
E. chown dave:staff data.txt










Answer E

When considering the use of hard links, what are valid reasons not to use hard links?
A. Hard links are not available on all Linux systems because traditional filesystems, such as ext4, do not support them- 
B. Each hard link has individual ownership, permissions and ACLs which can lead to unintended disclosure of file content- 
C. Hard links are specific to one filesystem and cannot point to files on another filesystem- 
D. If users other than root should be able to create hard links, suln has to be installed and configured- 
E. When a hard linked file is changed, a copy of the file is created and consumes additional space










Correct Answer: C

In compliance with the FHS, in which of the directories are man pages found?
A. /opt/man/- 
B. /usr/doc/- 
C. /usr/share/man/- 
D. /var/pkg/man- 
E. /var/man/










Correct Answer: C

Which file in the /proc filesystem lists parameters passed from the bootloader to the kernel? (Specify the file name only without any path.)









Correct Answer: cmdline

What is the process ID number of the init process on a System V init based system?
A. -1- 
B. 0- 
C. 1- 
D. It is different with each reboot- 
E. It is set to the current run level










Correct Answer: D

Which daemon handles power management events on a Linux system?

A. acpid
B. batteryd
C. pwrmgntd
D. psd
E. inetd










Correct Answer: A

Which of the following statements are true about the boot sequence of a PC using a BIOS? (Choose two.)

A. Some parts of the boot process can be configured from the BIOS
B. Linux does not require the assistance of the BIOS to boot a computer
C. The BIOS boot process starts only if secondary storage, such as the hard disk, is functional
D. The BIOS initiates the boot process after turning the computer on
E. The BIOS is started by loading hardware drivers from secondary storage, such as the hard disk








Correct Answer: AD



What is true regarding UEFI firmware? (Choose two.)

A. It can read and interpret partition tables
B. It can use and read certain file systems
C. It stores its entire configuration on the /boot/ partition
D. It is stored in a special area within the GPT metadata
E. It is loaded from a fixed boot disk position










Correct Answer: BD

A faulty kernel module is causing issues with a network interface card. Which of the following actions ensures that this module is not loaded automatically when the system boots?

A. Using lsmod –remove –autoclean without specifying the name of a specific module
B. Using modinfo -k followed by the name of the offending module
C. Using modprobe -r followed by the name of the offending module
D. Adding a blacklist line including the name of the offending module to the file /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf
E. Deleting the kernel module’s directory from the file system and recompiling the kernel, including its modules










Correct Answer: D

When is the content of the kernel ring buffer reset? (Choose two.)

A. When the ring buffer is explicitly reset using the command dmesg –clear
B. When the ring buffer is read using dmesg without any additional parameters
C. When a configurable amount of time, 15 minutes by default, has passed
D. When the kernel loads a previously unloaded kernel module
E. When the system is shut down or rebooted










Correct Answer: AE

What is the first program the Linux kernel starts at boot time when using System V init?

A. /lib/init.so
B. /proc/sys/kernel/init
C. /etc/rc.d/rcinit
D. /sbin/init
E. /boot/init










Correct Answer: D

A Debian package creates several files during its installation. Which of the following commands searches for packages owning the file /etc/debian_version?

A. apt-get search /etc/debian_version
B. apt -r /etc/debian_version
C. find /etc/debian_version -dpkg
D. dpkg -S /etc/debian_version
E. apt-file /etc/debian_version








Correct Answer: D



What is contained on the EFI System Partition?

A. The Linux root file system
B. The first stage boot loader
C. The default swap space file
D. The Linux default shell binaries
E. The user home directories








Correct Answer: B



Which of the following directories on a 64 bit Linux system typically contain shared libraries? (Choose two.)

A. ~/.lib64/
B. /usr/lib64/
C. /var/lib64/
D. /lib64/
E. /opt/lib64/








Correct Answer: BD



Which of the following files exist in a standard GRUB 2 installation? (Choose two.)

A. /boot/grub/stages/stage0
B. /boot/grub/i386-pc/lvm.mod
C. /boot/grub/fstab
D. /boot/grub/grub.cfg
E. /boot/grub/linux/vmlinuz








Correct Answer: BD



Which of the following commands installs all packages with a name ending with the string foo?

A. zypper get -*foo-
B. zypper update -foo?-
C. zypper force -foo*-
D. zypper install -*foo-
E. zypper add -.*foo-

Which of the following properties of a Linux system should be changed when a virtual machine is cloned? (Choose two.)

A. The partitioning scheme
B. The file system
C. The D-Bus Machine ID
D. The permissions of /root/

Which of the following commands installs GRUB 2 into the master boot record on the third hard disk?

Answer: The correct answer is gpasswd -a stewart wheel. The groupadd command is used to create a new group.

Which of the following addresses are by default in a class B network? Select all that apply.126.3.43.3128.1.3.7142.35.12.0190.3.78.3191.37.2.10193.24.2.43194.1.1.2

A host has the IP address 192.168.2.27 and a netmask of 255.255.255.0. What is the broadcast address of that network?









Anwser: 

You need to use tcpwrappers so that only address 192.168.1.7 can access all the services. Assuming you’ve added the appropriate entry to /etc/hosts.allow what would you need to put in /etc/hosts.deny? (or leave blank if you would not use hosts.deny)









Anwser: The answer isALL: ALL

Compose an entry for the /etc/syslog.conf file so that all debug messages go to a file “/var/log/debug”. Use spaces instead of the tab key.


The following is currently in the file:        # Kernel messages        kern.* -/var/log/kern.log        # Emergencies sent to everybody that is logged in        *.emerg *
    > Answer: The answer is:
    *.debug /var/log/debug
It is also valid with an = before the debug (only log debug, rather than debug and higher). It is also optional to have a hyphen in front of the path to indicate that the details will be cached.


Which is the CIDR netmask /27 in dotted decimal format?









Anwser: 255.255.255.224

Which of the following will allow a computer to make connections to the local X server, only if its ip address is 192.168.3.4? Select the most appropriate answer.xhost +xhost 192.168.3.4xhost -192.168.3.4xhost –allow=192.168.3.4

Which is the quaded decimal netmask 255.255.255.248 in CIDR notation.









Anwser: 29

If you want an interactive input from the user into a shell script what command can be used to get user input and store in in the $REPLY variable? Please enter the command only without any arguments.









Anwser: echo

After adding a new user what command will allow you to set the users initial password? Enter the command only, no options.









Anwser: the answer is passwd. The command will normally be followed by the users name such as: passwd username

Which of the following commands will show any pending at jobs? Select all that apply.at -laq -qatqatlat –list









Anwser: The correct answers are at -l and atq

Which of the following commands will create a new group called “trainers”? Select the most appropriate answer.addgroup trainersadd –group trainersgroupadd trainersnewgroup trainersnewgrp trainersThe correct answer is 









Answer: groupadd trainers. The newgrp command will change the current group during the session, not create a new one. The rest are not valid commands.

content:
Mondai 1繁盛 はんじょう buôn bán ,kinh doanh phát đạt 契約 けいやく hợp đồng
手薄 てうす lỏng lẻo,hời hợt
潤う うるおう giàu có,phát triển/ ẩm ướt 壊される こわされる bị phá bỏ
華々しい はなばなしい tráng lệ,rực rỡ

Mondai 2静止 せいし đứng yên và im lặng
成就 じょうじゅ thành tựu,thành quả
完結 かんけつ kết thúc
終息 しゅうそく dừng lại
キープ giữ,bảo vệ
フォロー hỗ trợ,giúp đỡ
マッチ phù hợp
念願 ねんがん cuối cùng cũng đạt được nguyện vọng 
本音 ý định thực sự
本気 nghiêm túc
弱音 よわね than thở
弱気 よわき nhát gan
やんわり khéo léo
しんなり mềm
ひんやり mát mẻ
当ホテル khách sạn này 
零細な れいさい tầm thường 
綿密な めんみつ tỉ mỉ 
繊細な せんさい tinh tế
濃密 のうみつ dày đặc

Mondai 3ルーズ = だらしない cẩu thả,bừa bãi
馴染む なじむ = 慣れる làm quen
張り合う = 競争 tranh đấu.
朗報 ろうほう = うれしい知らせ tin tốt lành,tin vui 
煩わしい = 面倒 phiền phức
嫌味 いやみ = 皮肉 ひにく chế giễu

Mondai 4密集 みっしゅう tập trung đông người この地区は古い住宅が密集 する。
潔い いさぎよい thẳng thắn,hiên ngang 間違ったことをしたと思うなら、潔く謝った 方がいいよ
発足 ほっそく thành lập この団体は先月発足したばかりです。
賑わう にぎわう đông đúc ,náo nhiệt 休みの前の日の夜はどこのレストランも賑わっている。
ひとまず trước tiên,tạm thời 今日の作業は ひとまず これでおわりにしよう。 
満喫 まんきつ thoả thích,tận hưởng 休暇を満喫した。

title: Từ vựng trong đề N1 qua các năm (7/️2011)
content:
Mondai 1利益 (ri-eki): Benefit, lợi nhuận
逃れる (nogareru): To escape, trốn tránh
考慮 (kō-ryō): Consideration, xem xét, suy tính
遮る (saegaru): To cover, to block, chắn, chặn đứng, cắt ngang
根拠 (kon-kyo): Basis, căn cứ
根拠に乏しい (kon-kyo ni tafushi-i): Lacking basis, thiếu căn cứ
肝心 (kan-jin): Essential, chủ yếu, quan trọng

Mondai 2N+ぐるみ (N+gurumi): Whole, the entirety, toàn bộ (gia đình), toàn thể (thành phố)
N+がらみ (N+garami): Involved, related to N, liên quan, dính dáng tới N
N+まみれ (N+mamire): Covered in N, toàn đầy là (cát, bụi, cặn bẩn, bùn...)
転がる (korogaru): To roll, to tumble, lăn lốc, ngã, sụp, đổ nhào
会話が弾む (kai-wa ga hajiku): Conversation is lively, cuộc trò chuyện thú vị
地域の事情 (chi-iki no jijō): Regional situation, tình hình khu vực
実況中継 (jikkyō chūkei): Live broadcast, truyền hình trực tiếp
実在 (jitsu-zai): Reality, thực tế
架空 (kakuu): Fictitious, giả tưởng
大家 (ōya): Expert, chuyên gia
おおや (ōya): Teacher, bậc thầy
玄人 (gen-jin): Expert, knowledgeable, chuyên gia, có chuyên môn
逸材 (itsusai): Outstanding talent, tài năng xuất chú
不当 ふとう (k chính đáng)
不備 ふび (không hoàn chỉnh, thiếu sót) 不順 ふじゅん (thời tiết thất thường)
強み つよみ (điểm mạnh)

Mondai 3画期的な かっきてきな (tính bước ngoặt, đột phá) 目論む めろもる (kế hoạch)
手がかり てがかり (manh mối, gợi ý)
にわかに にわかに (ngay lập tức) 重宝する じゅうほうする (tiện lợi)
シビア しびあ (khắt khe)

Mondai 4連携 れんけい (liên kết, hợp tác) 学校は地域と連携する。
不服 ふふく (không phục) 審判の判定に不服
叶う かなう (đáp ứng, thoả mãn (nguyện vọng, mong mỏi...)) 夢が叶う、お願いが叶う
目覚ましい めざましい (đáng kể, đầy ngạc nhiê
靴のひもがほどける くつのひもがほどける (dây giầy bị tuột) 
赴任 ふにん (việc tới nhận chức)
海外赴任 かいがいふにん (thực hiện việc ngoài nước)
